name: pymodbus (Modbus Protocol Implementation)

auth.notes: Local library; no auth — Modbus has no authentication

performance.notes: Network/serial latency dominates; async client enables concurrent requests

agent_readiness.notes: Modbus RTU/TCP/ASCII client and server. from pymodbus.client import ModbusTcpClient. client = ModbusTcpClient('192.168.1.100', port=502). client.connect(). rr = client.read_holding_registers(0, 10, unit=1). rr.registers → list of values. client.write_register(0, 100, unit=1). client.write_coil(0, True, unit=1). Async: from pymodbus.client import AsyncModbusTcpClient. async with AsyncModbusTcpClient('host') as client: rr = await client.read_holding_registers(0, 10). Serial RTU: ModbusSerialClient(method='rtu', port='/dev/ttyUSB0', baudrate=9600). Server: StartAsyncTcpServer(context). Register types: coils, discrete_inputs, holding_registers, input_registers.

gotchas: Modbus protocol has no authentication — never expose on untrusted networks
Unit ID (slave ID) required for RS-485 multidrop — easy to forget
Register scaling not built-in — must handle float/int conversion manually
API changed significantly in v3.x — older examples use deprecated sync API patterns
Function code support varies by device — not all devices support all registers
best_when: Industrial automation: reading PLCs, VFDs, energy meters via Modbus TCP/RTU
IoT gateway aggregating Modbus device data
avoid_when: When OPC-UA or PROFINET is the site standard
Secure industrial systems (Modbus has no auth — use secured OPC-UA)
summary: pymodbus implements Modbus RTU/TCP/ASCII client and server. ModbusTcpClient('host').read_holding_registers(0, 10). write_register(), write_coil(). Async: AsyncModbusTcpClient. Serial RTU: ModbusSerialClient(method='rtu', port='/dev/ttyUSB0'). Server: StartAsyncTcpServer. BSD-3. Best for PLC/VFD/energy meter SCADA integration. No authentication in Modbus protocol — internal networks only. API changed in v3.x. Manual float/int register conversion needed.
